summary: Matched a miscellaneous batch of game-mode code, centered on event/minor-scene logic in gm_1BA8, result-display helpers in gm_17BA, and one debug/game-over leave function in gm_19EF. The PR also tightened several prototypes and struct fields in gm/types headers, added data-format annotations for byte and Shift-JIS objects in symbols.txt, and promoted gm_17BA.c from NonMatching to Matching. decomp.dev reported 37 new matches, +7388 matched-code bytes, and gm_17BA.c becoming a matching object.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_1BA8 functions, including event initialization/load flow, event objective/state updates, score/timer handling, and minor-scene data transfer helpers such as gm_801BBA60_OnInit, gm_801BBEA8_OnLoad, gm_801BC4F4, gm_801BC670, gm_801BC754, gm_801BCAF0, gm_801BD164, gm_801BD658, gm_801BD93C, gm_801BDD44, gm_801BED3C, and gm_801BEE58.
Added gm_803DF618_MinorScenes in gm_1BA8.c and moved/declared supporting gm_804D6900 static data layout into the new gm_1BA8.static.h instead of including gm_1B03.static.h.
Added a local byte table gm_803DF918 in gm_1BA8.c and marked the corresponding symbols.txt object as data:byte.

Implemented all visible gm_17BA stubs: fn_8017BACC and fn_8017BB30 clamp signed result values to +/-999999, while fn_8017BB94/fn_8017BC50/fn_8017BD0C/fn_8017BDC8 return per-target player labels or persistent-name data.
Added Japanese full-width player label arrays in gm_17BA.c and selected between labels based on lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS().
Marked melee/gm/gm_17BA.c as Matching in configure.py.

Implemented gm_801A0B18_OnLeave in gm_19EF.c, copying fields from a static data struct into DebugGameOverData, stopping/freeing audio/archive resources through lbAudioAx_800236DC and lbArchive_80016EFC.
Expanded struct gmm_x0 event data in types.h with bitfields and concrete members at offsets 0x53B through 0x580, including timer-related x2C/x30 and arrays x4C/x50.
Changed MatchPlayerData.x9C and xA0 from u32 to int, matching the new signed clamping logic in gm_17BA.
Refined DebugGameOverData.x4 from padding to an int field used by gm_801A0B18_OnLeave.

Tightened several prototypes: gm_80169384 returns int instead of u8, gm_8016AEEC returns u32 instead of int, gm_80164A0C takes int, gm_801A0A10_OnEnter and gm_801A0B18_OnLeave take void*, ifStock_802FA2D0 takes int, lbAudioAx_80028B90 takes void, and several gm_1BA8.h prototypes now have concrete argument/return types.
Added gm_17AD.h and gm_17BA.h exports to gm_unsplit.h.
Annotated eight sdata labels lbl_804D4010 through lbl_804D4048 as data:sjis in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
smart_moves: Factored the four nearly identical gm_17BA player-name functions through a static inline helper, preserving separate exported wrappers while reducing duplicate source logic.
Used explicit signed int fields for MatchPlayerData.x9C/xA0 so the +/-999999 clamp code naturally matches signed comparisons instead of forcing awkward casts.
Added PAD_STACK calls in wrapper-style functions where the matched assembly needed stack shape preserved.
Introduced a file-specific static header, gm_1BA8.static.h, to describe archive-loaded/static data dependencies without polluting broader headers.
Converted unknown padding in the event-data struct into named bitfields and offset-specific fields only where the new matched functions demonstrated actual use.
Updated symbol data encodings to byte and sjis, helping the linker/data pipeline represent tables and full-width Japanese strings correctly.
Kept gm_17BA exported functions named by address while improving prototypes to const char* or int, balancing current naming uncertainty with useful type information.
decomp_lessons: When multiple address-specific functions only differ by a small constant target index, a static inline helper plus thin wrappers can satisfy matching constraints while keeping source maintainable.
Result-screen score fields may need signed types even when prior placeholders used u32; check for negative clamps and signed threshold behavior before finalizing struct members.
Data annotations in symbols.txt matter for matching: byte tables and Shift-JIS string labels should be declared with data:byte or data:sjis instead of leaving them as generic objects.
Header prototype cleanup can unlock matches in neighboring files because argument count, return width, and signedness affect generated code.
Large event/minor-scene functions often require reconstructing the backing save/event struct first; bitfields at single-byte flag offsets can be necessary for clean matches.
File-local static headers are useful for partially known archive data layouts that are needed by one C file but are not mature enough for a public subsystem header.
Use exact floating literal style where needed for matching; this diff changed 1.0f to 1.0F in gm_801BF4DC.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for unknown game-mode functions, e.g. gm_801BC754, gm_801BCAF0, fn_8017BACC, and gm_801A0B18_OnLeave.
Lifecycle functions use suffixes such as _OnInit, _OnLoad, _OnUnload, _OnEnter, and _OnLeave.
Minor scene arrays use the suffix _MinorScenes, as in gm_803DF618_MinorScenes.
Local/global data symbols follow address-based names such as gm_803DF918, gm_803DF94C, lbl_804D6698, and lbl_80479A98 when semantic names are not yet established.
Struct fields remain offset-style xN names where semantics are incomplete, with occasional comments such as x2C // timer seconds.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked gm_17BA.c Matching in configure.py after replacing stubs with matched implementations.
Used PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(0x18) in functions where stack padding was needed for matching.
Declared gm_803DF918 as a u8 array and changed its symbol to data:byte to match emitted data layout.
Tagged full-width player label sdata as data:sjis and added explicit static const char* arrays for Japanese/US label variants.
Used signed clamp boundaries of 999999 and -999999 in gm_17BA result helpers to match result-screen behavior and comparison codegen.
Reconstructed bitfield flags xB_0 through xB_7 in the event data struct to produce direct flag assignments used by gm_1BA8 functions.
Adjusted return types and argument types across headers to avoid placeholder UNK_PARAMS/UNK_T codegen mismatches.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only visible comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing 37 new matches, +7388 matched-code bytes, and +952 linked-code bytes.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename gm_1BA8 event data fields xB_0..x50 to semantic event-mode names.
Find follow-up work on gm_801BC00C, gm_801BC488, gm_801BEE9C, and other remaining gm_1BA8 stubs.
Search for subsequent PRs that give semantic names to gm_17BA result-screen helpers and MatchPlayerData.x9C/xA0.
Check whether gm_1BA8.static.h data structures were later moved into a public gm/event header.
Search for more symbols.txt data:sjis annotations around lbl_804D4010-lbl_804D4048 and other Japanese UI labels.
